title: Effective scatterings and universal clusters of heteronuclear ultracold mixtures in quasi-low dimensions
abstract: We study the effective s-wave scattering of two heteronuclear atoms harmonically confined in quasi-low dimensions, where the atoms have unequal masses and are subject to different confinement frequencies. The resulting effective scattering parameters in low dimensions, including scattering length and effective range, are derived as functions of three-dimensional scattering parameters and confinement strengths. Using realistic Li-K and Li-Cr mixtures as examples, we further compute the binding energies of universal $(1+N)$ clusters in quasi-low dimensions using the effective scattering parameters. Our findings suggest a promising pathway for practically observing universal clusters and their associated many-body phases in low-dimensional ultracold heteronuclear systems.
summary: This theoretical paper investigates the effective scattering properties and cluster binding energies of heteronuclear ultracold atomic mixtures confined in quasi-low dimensions. By solving the scattering problem in 1D and 2D using advanced techniques like the Lippmann-Schwinger equation, the authors predict conditions favorable for observing universal few-body quantum phases in experiments.
main_problem: The study aims to derive the effective s-wave scattering parameters and compute the binding energies of universal $(1+N)$ clusters in heteronuclear ultracold mixtures confined in quasi-low dimensions.
main_result: The calculations provide a theoretical pathway for practically observing universal clusters and their associated many-body phases in low-dimensional ultracold heteronuclear systems.
method: The authors employ the Lippmann-Schwinger equation framework, solving coupled scattering problems in 2D and 1D geometries, and utilizing matrix diagonalization techniques to handle divergences.
model_or_system: The system involves two heteronuclear atoms (unequal masses) harmonically confined in quasi-low dimensions (q1D and q2D), exemplified by Li-K and Li-Cr mixtures.
key_observables: Effective 2D/1D scattering lengths ($a_{2D}, a_{1D}$) and effective ranges ($R_{2D}, R_{1D}$), and the binding energies of $(1+N)$ clusters ($\Delta_{1+N,N}$).
important_parameters: Confinement strengths, 3D scattering parameters, and the ratio $R^*/a_s$ are critical parameters governing the effective low-dimensional physics.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on approximations such as decoupling the transverse center-of-mass motion for 2D, and using specific regularization techniques (e.g., short-time/long-time cutoffs) to handle divergences.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the effective scattering parameters and the binding energies of two-body and few-body clusters for Li-Cr and Li-K systems in both quasi-2D and quasi-1D geometries.
paper_structure: The paper proceeds by first deriving the effective low-dimensional scattering parameters using the Lippmann-Schwinger equation for 2D, then extending the analysis to 1D, and finally applying these effective parameters to calculate the binding energies of universal clusters.
why_interesting: This work directly addresses the theoretical modeling of few-body physics in highly controllable quantum systems, providing quantitative predictions for the observation of universal many-body states in reduced dimensions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Quasi-2D effective scattering and bound states for 6Li-53Cr mixture, under longitudinal harmonic confinement with equal confinement length lho = (mhωh)−1/2 = (mlωl)−1/2. (a, b) The reduced 2D scattering length a2D and effective range R2D as functions of R∗/as. Here, as and R∗= 6000a0 are respectively the 3D scattering length and effective range. (c) Two-body binding energy E2 and relative binding energies ∆1+N,N ≡E1+N −EN of (1 + N) clusters (with N = 2, 3) as functions of R∗/as. For E2, discrete data and solid line are respectively from exact solution and effective 2D model; For ∆32 and ∆43, they are all from effective 2D model. (d) Relative ratios |∆1+N,N/E2| (N = 2, 3) as...
FIG. 2. The same as Fig.1 except for 6Li-40K mixture with effective range R∗= 2400a0.

FIG. 3. Quasi-1D effective scattering and bound states for 6Li-53Cr mixture, under transverse harmonic confinement with equal confinement length lho = (mhωh)−1/2 = (mlωl)−1/2. (a, b) The reduced 1D scattering length a1D and effective range R1D as functions of R∗/as. (c) Two-body binding energy E2 and relative binding energies ∆1+N,N ≡E1+N −EN of (1 + N) clusters (with N = 2, 3) as functions of R∗/as. For E2, discrete data and solid line are respectively from exact solution and effective 2D model; For ∆32 and ∆43, they are all from effective 2D model. (d) Relative ratios |∆1+N,N/E2| (N = 2, 3) as functions of R∗/as. In all plots, R∗= 6000a0 and we take three different lho = 1500, 1000,...
FIG. 4. The same as Fig.3 except for 6Li-40K mixture with effective range R∗= 2400a0.
